title: Alcubierre warp drive — boundary-mode reformulation: notebooks, sweeps, and verification artifacts
description:
Personal landscape exploration of whether the exotic-matter requirement of the Alcubierre warp-drive metric can be reinterpreted as a boundary effect (Casimir analog) or evaded via classical anisotropic matter shells (Fuchs et al. 2024 anchor).

This deposit archives the full research repository at the tagged release: SymPy/NumPy notebooks deriving the linearization and slice geometry, parameter-sweep modules (run on Hugging Face Jobs and locally), independent MATLAB / Warp Factory numerical verification, evaluation companions for cited papers (Rodal 2025, Krasnikov 2003, Fell-Heisenberg 2021), the static-research website rendering the results, and the trust-audit / session-log / navigator documents that record provenance and slice scope of every claim.

Honest framing: all results are reported with their slice of parameter space (which assumptions they depend on). The composite finding so far is "within the static slice plus six relaxations explored to date, no useful classical warp drive exists," which is informative but does not close the broader question. See NAVIGATOR.md, TRUST_AUDIT.md, and LANDSCAPE_SYNTHESIS.md in the archive for the full provenance audit.

Companion live dataset on Hugging Face: bshepp/alcubierre-sweeps. Source repository: github.com/bshepp/alcubierre.
